# terrible_cisco_mso_ndo_virtual_port_channel_interface (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) The description of the Virtual Port Channel Interface.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interface_descriptions` (String) The list of interface descriptions of the Virtual Port Channel Interface. Providing a new list of `interface_descriptions` will completely replace an existing one from the Virtual Port Channel Interface. Providing an empty list will remove the `interface_descriptions=[]` from the Virtual Port Channel Interface.
- `interface_policy_group` (String) The Port Channel Interface Policy Group. This parameter can be used instead of `interface_policy_group_uuid`.
- `interface_policy_group_uuid` (String) The UUID of the Port Channel Interface Policy Group. This parameter is required when creating a new Virtual Port Channel Interface.
- `interfaces_node_1` (String) The list of used Interface IDs for the first node. Ranges of Interface IDs can be used. This parameter is required when creating a new Virtual Port Channel Interface.
- `interfaces_node_2` (String) The list of used Interface IDs for the second node. Ranges of Interface IDs can be used. This parameter is required when creating a new Virtual Port Channel Interface. If `interfaces_node_2=[mirror]` and `interfaces_node_1` is clearly defined, the interfaces of `interfaces_node_1` will be mirrored in `interfaces_node_2`.
- `name` (String) The name of the Virtual Port Channel Interface.
- `node_1` (String) The first node ID.
- `node_2` (String) The second node ID.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Use `absent` for removing. Use `query` for listing an object or multiple objects. Use `present` for creating or updating.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `template` (String) The name of the Fabric Resource template. This parameter or `template_id` is required.
- `template_id` (String) The ID of the Fabric Policy template. This parameter or `template` is required.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the Virtual Port Channel Interface. This parameter can be used instead of `virtual_port_channel_interface` when an existing Virtual Port Channel Interface is updated. This parameter is required when parameter `name` is updated.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
